sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 4.39 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.4)

statement.latex: A countable group$U$is said to be${ SQ$-uni\-ver\-sal\/} if every countable group is isomorphic to a subgroup of a quotient group of~$U$. Let$G$be a group that has a presentation with$r 2$generators and at most$r-2$defining relations. Is$G$\${ SQ}$-uni\-ver\-sal? { A.\,M.\,Macbeath,~P.\,M.\,Neumann} Yes, it is (B.\,Baumslag, S.\,J.\,Pride, J. London Math. Soc., { 17} (1978), 425--426).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
